advantages: Produces a valid probability distribution over mutually exclusive classes (positive, sums to 1).
Differentiable end-to-end with cross-entropy, enabling standard gradient-based training of deep classifiers.
Order-preserving: the highest logit always gets the highest probability, matching intuitive ranking.
Shift-invariant and amenable to log-sum-exp — stable log-probability computation at scale.
Natural parameterization of the Categorical distribution, connecting outputs to maximum-likelihood training.
disadvantages: Mutually exclusive normalization: raising π_k lowers all other π_j — ill-suited for multi-label tasks where labels are independent.
Can become overconfident (π near 0 or 1) without regularization such as label smoothing or temperature scaling.
Computes probabilities over all K classes — expensive when K is huge (e.g. million-class), though sampling and hierarchical softmax mitigate this.
Sensitive to logit scale: very large logits yield near one-hot outputs and sharp gradients that can destabilize early training.
Argmax prediction ignores calibrated probability quality — a model can be accurate yet poorly calibrated on softmax outputs.
